#Summary1=(human1) The Montana Supreme Court heard arguments in “Rodney Brandt et al. v. R&R Mountain Escapes LLC”, about contract interpretation and property rights, with Stephanie Baucus arguing on behalf of the appellants that rentals are residential and ought to have protected property rights, while justices questioned if short term rentals violate residential property covenants.

#Summary2=(claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929; postedited) Stephanie Baucus, representing appellants Rodney Brandt and others in a Montana Supreme Court case about restrictive covenants, argues that the covenants unambiguously permit short-term rentals because they expressly allow rentals without specifying duration and do not prohibit the residential use guests engage in during their stays.

#Summary3=(gpt4o) In the Montana Supreme Court case of Rodney Brandt vs. R & R Mountain Escapes LLC, attorney Stephanie Baucus argued for the appellants, asserting that the covenants allowing rentals have no duration language, making them unambiguous and challenging the district court's decision as potentially detrimental to property rights and residential rentals in Montana.

#Summary4=(Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct; postedited) Appellants' attorney Stephanie Baucus argues that the Montana Supreme Court should uphold a property owner's right to rent their home short-term, citing a covenant that explicitly allows rentals without specifying duration.

#Summary5=(Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct; postedited) Stephanie Baucus argues for appellants Rodney Brandt and others, contending that the restrictive covenants permit rentals and that short-term rentals do not constitute commercial use, emphasizing the importance of upholding property rights in Montana.
#Text=All rise and give your attention to the honorable justices of the Montana Supreme Court .
#Text=Now in session .
#Text=Good morning everybody .
#Text=This is the time set for oral argument in cause number DA 23-0716 .
#Text=Rodney Brandt and others versus R & R Mountain Escapes LLC .
#Text=So I , uh , understand Mr. Frampton will be arguing for the the appellants .
#Text=Is that correct ?
#Text=Sorry .
#Text=Appellees .
#Text=And Miss Baucus will be arguing for the appellants .
#Text=And then I have a note here that you want to split your time , uh , saving 15 minutes for rebuttal .
#Text=Is that accurate ?
#Text=Yep , that 'd be fine .
#Text=All right .
#Text=Ready to proceed .
#Text=May it please the court .
#Text=And good morning .
#Text=I 'm Stephanie Baucus here on behalf of the appellants .
#Text=R & R Mountain Escapes , uh who are -- it 's completely owned by Doctor Russell Palmer and Ramona Stewart .
#Text=Ramona Stewart is here with us today .
#Text=Also here are amicus parties who have filed briefs in support of the Palmers , including the Landlords ' Association and the Bridger Canyon Property Rights Coalition .

#Text=Thank you for inviting us here to address these important issues for our state , which involve important questions of contract interpretation , the scope of property rights , and contract construction .

#Text=Not only are these questions important for the Palmers and the plaintiffs , they are important for Montana homeowners and tenants because residential homes in our state , most of them have some form of residential covenants that apply to them .
#Text=I 'd like to emphasize three main points in my argument today .
#Text=First , there are two undisputed facts in this case that resolve the case in appellants ' favor .
#Text=First , the covenants allow rentals , and second , the covenants have no language about duration .
#Text=We contend that this makes the covenants unambiguous .
#Text=Second , our theory of the case is consistent with precedent , including Craig Tracts and its progeny like Meyers v Kleinhans , whether or not the court finds the covenants ambiguous .
#Text=And third , this case is critical to upholding Montana 's historic protection of fundamental property rights .
#Text=Not only is rental use one of the sticks in the bundle of property rights , but short term rental use has been around since the founding of our country .
#Text=And if the district court 's decision is not reversed , this case could have wide sweeping and destructive impacts for thousands of Montana homeowners in the rental context and otherwise .
#Text=Upon the first point , there are two critical and disputed facts .
#Text=First , we think these facts make the covenants unambiguous .
#Text=But at a minimum , they create an ambiguity that must be resolved in appellant 's favor .
#Text=Unlike the court 's recent decisions in Craig Tracts or Brown -- Craig Tracts v Brown Drake or Meyers v Kleinhans , in this case , the restrictive covenants at issue permit rentals .
#Text=The terms expressly contemplate rentals in the section A6 , which is about advertising , and it 's talking about signs .
#Text=It allows signs , advertising billboards , and advertising structures for sale or rent .
#Text=And the plaintiffs themselves use their property for long term rental .
#Text=Could I interrupt you ?
#Text=Of course .
#Text=So the covenants also say only for residential purposes , not commercial .
#Text=Um , how do you address that ?
#Text=I understand your argument with respect to the rentals , but the covenants also say not for anything commercial .
#Text=And Meyers .
#Text=So , Your Honor , thank you for the question .

#Text=Um , we certainly contend that the short term rentals that the Palmers have been doing , um , which were a minimum five night stay , the Palmers themselves stayed in the property for eight weeks of the year , um , 27 nights maximum that they had rented out in 23 .
#Text=Um , those uses , when the renters are there , are the same as they would be when the Palmers are there .
#Text=They 're eating and bathing and sleeping , recreating , relaxing , um , potentially doing a little bit --
#Text=How is that not commercial ?
#Text=I mean , we found in Kleinhans -- Kleinhans that short term rentals are commercial use that violate a covenant , that says no commercial use .
#Text=So I know that we have the rental covenant .
#Text=Then we have the no commercial use covenant .
#Text=How do you weave them together to give both of them meaning ?
#Text=Right .
#Text=Well , so the definitions that the court looked at and the set of covenants in Meyers v Kleinhans were different than they are in this case .
#Text=In this case , we have a specific set of provisions , one of which is to expressly allow rentals without any reference to duration .

#Text=And so if you 're going to read the covenant and the contract as a whole , which is required under standard principles of contract instruction -- contract construction , you have to do that in a way that makes those provisions harmonize .
#Text=And in order to do that , you need to look at what the drafter intended by the definition of commercial .
#Text=And so the definitions that this court looked to in the Meyers v Kleinhans case would also prohibit long term rentals in this case .
#Text=And we know that that 's not going to be possible because there is a provision that contemplates rentals .
#Text=So the type of commercial activities are further defined by the drafter 's intent .
#Text=You can see in the provision regarding commercial use where it talks about ' such as ' it says , that commercial uses are prohibited , it says " such as junkyards and mobile home parks " .
#Text=But it also says " etc. "
#Text=Right ?
#Text=Correct .
#Text=And also it says " whatsoever " .
#Text=So I mean no commercial use whatsoever .
#Text=It seems very broad to me .
#Text=I mean , to get the intention .
#Text=The appellants do n't disagree that the commercial use prohibition is somewhat broad , but it ca n't be read to prohibit rentals .

#Text=The plaintiffs and the defendants or appellants and appellees both agree that long term rentals are permitted um under the covenants , and there 's no difference between a rental of 28 days and a rental of 31 days .
#Text=The court in the district um the district court pulled a 30 day limitation out , and it created that limitation onto the contract .
#Text=There is nothing in the contract that talks about that .
#Text=Um , and again , the rental use that is being done by the guests or the renters that the Palmers have at their property , um , was the same as it would be no matter what the duration was .
#Text=Let me follow up on that , because I think they they make the point that that 's regulatory based on the county 's permit .
#Text=And I 'll , we 'll address that later .
#Text=But I want to go back to what Justice Gustafson was talking about .
#Text=So the prohibition is any other commercial ?
#Text=It does n't say use .
#Text=It says purpose , " prohibits any other commercial purpose whatsoever . "
#Text=So whatsoever is a pretty broad term .
